location.office_time_required: some in person meetings, still allows for flexibility to work from home

level_signals: Platform / data infrastructure focus suggests need for some technical maturity but relatively low minimum bar
No senior or staff designation in title

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 2+ years of Product Management experience, ideally working on platform products, developer tools, data infrastructure, analytics platforms, or data governance systems.
You have a technical foundation through a degree in Computer Science, Engineering, a related field, and/or prior experience in engineering or technical roles.
You are comfortable working with engineers and can engage in technical discussions while representing customer and business needs.
You are an excellent communicator and collaborator who can build alignment across teams with different priorities and levels of technical expertise.
You have strong systems-thinking skills and can connect workflows, platforms, users, and business outcomes.
You have empathy for people who work with data and understand the challenges they face every day.
You enjoy working through ambiguity, breaking down complex problems, and creating clarity for teams.
You are curious about how AI is transforming the data landscape and are excited to help shape that future.
You lead with humility, build trust through collaboration, and help teams move forward with focus and momentum.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 2+ years of Product Management experience, ideally working on platform products, developer tools, data infrastructure, analytics platforms, or data governance systems.

scope.surface: Spotify's data catalogue, metadata, and data lineage capabilities within the Data Platform Studio
primary_metrics_inferred: Adoption of data catalogue and metadata capabilities
Value creation and business impact of data platform investments
Data discovery, trust, and governance KPIs
core_responsibilities: Develop and own the product strategy and roadmap for Spotify's data catalogue, metadata, and data lineage domain.
Diagnose and deeply understand the needs of Spotify's data community, including data engineers, data scientists, analysts, ML engineers, and increasingly AI agents.
Identify and prioritize the highest-impact problems to solve, balancing user needs, business priorities, platform requirements, and regulatory considerations.
Evaluate and deliver solutions through building, buying, or integrating third-party products and capabilities.
Define success metrics and KPIs that measure adoption, value creation, and business impact.
Build strong relationships with internal product, engineering, analytics, and data teams to understand workflows, gather feedback, and shape future platform investments.
Collaborate closely with engineering, design, and data science partners to deliver high-quality platform experiences.
Influence stakeholders across the Data Platform Studio and broader Spotify organization to align around product direction and priorities.
Help shape how Spotify approaches metadata, governance, discoverability, lineage, and trust in an increasingly AI-driven world.
Stay informed on industry trends across data platforms, developer tools, AI/ML infrastructure, analytics, and data governance.

green_flags: Clear product surface and domain ownership (data catalogue, metadata, lineage) — not vague
Hybrid flexibility explicitly stated with ability to work from home
Role sits at a strategically important infrastructure domain ('center of one of the most important infrastructure domains at Spotify')
Encourages candidates with equivalent experience in lieu of formal degree
red_flags: Compensation not disclosed anywhere in the JD
Visa sponsorship and work authorization requirements not mentioned — unclear for international candidates

2+ years minimum is low for the strategic scope described (own strategy and roadmap, influence org-wide stakeholders), suggesting either a wide candidate band or potential misalignment between title and responsibilities
No indication of team size or reporting structure

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from '2+ years' minimum and mid-level title; no explicit level stated)
scope.business_model (inferred as internal platform from JD context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed as KPIs in JD)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as false since role is stated as Toronto-based)
ambiguities: The 'ideally working on platform products, developer tools, data infrastructure...' qualifier in the years-of-experience requirement is ambiguous — it reads as a soft preference attached to a hard minimum. Treated as: 2+ years PM = hard requirement; platform/data domain context = nice-to-have.
Work authorization and visa sponsorship are not addressed anywhere in the JD; left as 'unclear'.
Office time requirement is described vaguely ('some in person meetings') with no percentage or days-per-week specified.
